unmet_need: Personalized guidance and contextual recommendations to facilitate music discovery
segment: Casual listeners and busy individuals seeking effortless discovery
example_quotes: Chévere😘. Me encantan mi playlist sin Spotify me vuelvo loca😂🩷
functionality to chose and support versions, I hear the same songs multiple times on shuffle.
Stuck on repeat with the same comfort songs. The app never gently nudges me to expand my rotation.

unmet_need: Personalized music recommendations that go beyond familiar artists and genres
segment: Music enthusiasts seeking new artists and genres
example_quotes: LOVE IT. I love that I can listen to all my favorite artists and songs and make playlist for free!
why do i save 10 songs off release radar and then never listen to them again. nothing ever sticks lol
Same here. The algorithm keeps recommending the same five artists over and over. It feels like an echo chamber, not discovery.

unmet_need: Effortless introduction to new music that fits their existing taste, with features to help retain and incorporate new artists into their regular listening habits
example_quotes: as a busy parent i have zero time to crate dig. i just want good new music to come to me with no effort
Busy parents need zero-effort discovery that respects their time and comes to them instead of making them dig.
New music never sticks for me. I play a recommended song once and forget it — it never makes it into my rotation.

unmet_need: Personalized and adventurous music discovery that caters to individual tastes and preferences
example_quotes: give me a dial for how adventurous my recs are. some days i want comfort, some days i want a real stretch
Grounded 'why this' bridges build trust. Recommendations without a reason feel random and I never act on them.
A comfort-to-curiosity dial is genius. The biggest gap in every recommender is control over how far the recs stretch.

unmet_need: Users want to discover new and diverse music, but the current algorithm only recommends familiar or repetitive artists and genres
segment: Music enthusiasts and power users who rely on Discover Weekly for new music discovery
example_quotes: My discover weekly is stale. Every week it's the same vibe and the same handful of artists. I'm bored of it.
discover weekly used to find me artists i'd never have found myself. now it's all safe picks i already know. discovery is broken
I open Discover Weekly, save a couple songs, and forget them by next week. High intent, nothing sticks. Story of my Spotify life.

unmet_need: ability to filter out AI-generated music from recommendations
segment: music enthusiasts/purists who value human-created content
example_quotes: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs. Ai music has ruined my Discover Weekly. Looked forward to it every week  😢
Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs. Always create your own playlists, folks. Discover music through other sites instead

Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs. I don’t post to stuff like this. Gang….if I can’t filter AI content soon, I’m out. Have loved the service for several years now, but I can’t even listen to my discover weekly anymore.

topic: No guided on-ramp into a new genre
unmet_need: Staged, goal-based exploration that coaches you into a genre
example_quotes: Re: i feel like all the playlists now sound the same and don't find new music. If you want to discover, you might want to listen my playlist.   It soon hits 10 000 songs on Spotify.   https://open.spotify.com/playlist/2WqJBJQjrFpeztv5AiS6PG?si=862bd9e694354a56   You might want to try new playlists, in new genre, to hear something new and special

Re: Let's introduce ourselves!. Hi @SunnyIsNotOkay , welcome to the Community 😀   I actually discover tracks like "垮" fairly regularly through my playlist curation. 'ASA Wu' is a hidden gem. 'Garden' from her latest EP and 'Saturday Afternoon' would sit next to "垮" like gold and platinum.    Perfect timing too  - Trooper Salute released a new album today called "友達がいました". Your favorite artist has a busy week ahead 😍   Good luck with your courses! May the semester end better than you're predicting.  Kia ora from New Zealand. Welcome 😀

Improve the big green play button in playlists. This is the scenario. I open the desktop app, and then click on a playlist like Discover Weekly. There's a big green button at the top of this view - what do you think happens if you press it?    Logically, it should mean the user intends to play this playlist. But that's not what it does. This just resumes whatever was playing last time you used Spotify.    There are actually 2 play buttons in the UI in this user journey. The white one at the bottom could resume where the user left off last time. The green one in the playlist, should play the playlist.
